commit.message:
fix(starrocks): increase operator memory limits to prevent OOMKill

The operator was being OOMKilled during FE leader election reconciliation.
The previous 128Mi limit was insufficient for the reconciliation loop.

Raise the request to 256Mi so the operator is scheduled on a node with
adequate headroom, and raise the limit to 512Mi to give the reconciler
enough burst capacity without risking node-level memory pressure.

Fix: Reduce polling interval and add request deduplication

Problem:
- Frontend was polling backend every 500ms
- Each request triggered 3+ second Renko calculation
- 500ms interval meant 6+ requests stacking up
- Thread pool exhaustion caused WebSocket disconnections
- Backend couldn't keep up with request volume

Solution:
- Increased polling interval from 500ms to 2000ms (2 seconds)
- Added isPendingRef to skip duplicate requests while one is in flight
- Prevents request stacking and thread pool exhaustion
- Allows backend calculations to complete before next poll

Result:
- Backend stays responsive
- WebSocket connections stable
- No more "Error sending personal message"
- Cleaner request pattern (1 request per 2 seconds, not 4x per calc)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement draw() loop with motion-blur, force, update, wrap and render steps (tasks 5.3 + 5.4)

Implements the main simulation draw loop in the correct order:
1. background(0,0,0,25) — semi-transparent fade produces motion-blur trail
2. applyForces() — O(n²) toroidal force calculation, returns bond pairs
3. tickFluxar() per particle — advances chaos clock (flips chaosSign every 120 frames)
4. updateParticles() — integrates acceleration into velocity with DAMPING, clips to maxSpeed, moves positions
5. wrapPosition() per particle — toroidal boundary wrapping
6. drawBondLines() then drawParticle() — bond lines drawn first so particles render on top

drawParticle and drawBondLines are added as minimal stubs; task 6.1 adds outer-glow,
task 6.2 adds Vortaar rotation, and task 6.3 enhances bond-line styling.
Tasks 5.3 and 5.4 are implemented together since 5.4 specifies exactly how the
fade-background step of 5.3 works — they are inseparable.

commit.message:
Add king travel system — royal progress and diplomatic visits

Kings can now embark on two types of trips:
- Royal Progress: tour own kingdom towns (up to 3), boosting happiness
  and prosperity in each visited town. Prioritizes troubled/large towns.
- Diplomatic Visit: travel to a foreign capital to improve relations.
  Weighted toward allies and friendly kingdoms. Blocked during war.

Travel decisions are personality+mood driven:
- Ambitious/brave/kind kings travel more often
- Paranoid/fearful/grieving kings never leave the capital
- Cowardly/lazy/content kings rarely travel
- Wrathful kings mostly stay home

Safety rules ensure king is home when needed:
- Returns immediately when war is declared (diplomatic trips)
- Returns for royal tournaments
- Won't travel during succession crisis or low treasury (<2000g)
- 60-day cooldown between trips (king is home ~75%+ of the time)
- Daily gold cost for travel (5g/day progress, 10g/day diplomatic)

Config: CONFIG.KING_TRAVEL with all tunable parameters.
UI: Travel status shown in kingdom detail panel and world overview.
Help: New 'King Travel' entry in help system.
Save compat: kingTravel and _lastTravelEndDay added to load defaults.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(starrocks): encrypt shared-data bucket with data-analytics KMS key

The shared-data S3 bucket was created with default SSE-S3 encryption.
Switch to the same KMS key used by all other lakehouse buckets
(kms_s3_data_analytics_key from the KMS stack) with bucket-key enabled
to reduce per-request KMS call overhead.

Also extend the IRSA inline policy with kms:GenerateDataKey and
kms:Decrypt on the key ARN so FE/CN pods can read and write encrypted
objects without hitting AccessDenied from KMS.

feat(starrocks): create shared-data S3 bucket and wire fe.conf for CN mode

CN (shared-storage) mode requires an S3 bucket for persistent cluster state:
tablet data, metadata snapshots, and compaction output.  Without it the FE
reports no available capacity and all queries fail.

Changes:
- Create OLBucket named ol-starrocks-<env_prefix>-<env_suffix> when use_cn=true
  (e.g. ol-starrocks-lakehouse-qa, ol-starrocks-lakehouse-production)
- Attach inline IAM policy to the IRSA role granting s3:GetObject/PutObject/
  DeleteObject on the bucket objects and s3:ListBucket/GetBucketLocation on
  the bucket itself
- Unify fe.conf building into _build_fe_config() which composes SSL and
  shared-data sections independently; extends the chart-version guard to also
  cover the CN path since both sections rely on the same base config
- fe.conf shared-data additions (all static params requiring FE restart):
    run_mode = shared_data
    cloud_native_meta_port = 6090
    cloud_native_storage_type = S3
    aws_s3_path = <bucket>
    aws_s3_region = <region>
    aws_s3_endpoint = https://s3.<region>.amazonaws.com
    aws_s3_use_instance_profile = true   (covers both EC2 profile and IRSA)
    enable_load_volume_from_conf = true  (must be explicit; default=false in 3.4.1+)
- Export shared_data_bucket_name stack output for downstream reference
- Hoist aws_region read to module scope (was duplicated inside enable_data_lake block)
- Add json import (used by inline IAM policy)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message: Move k-means implementation from diskann-providers to diskann-disk (#933) K-means in `diskann-providers` was the last consumer of the old BLAS-based clustering path; PQ training has since migrated to `diskann-quantization`. The only active call site remaining was disk-index partitioning in `diskann-disk`. We will keep diskann-providers's implementation for now and move it to `diskann-disk`, rather than switching to the one in diskann-quantization, for the following reasons: - K-means in diskann-providers performs better at higher dimensions (>100): - K-means in diskann-providers supports multi-threading: We will work on closing these performance gaps and converging the two implementations in separate PRs. # Changes in this PR ## diskann-disk - Added `src/utils/kmeans.rs` — k-means implementation moved from `diskann-providers` - Added `src/utils/math_util.rs` — mathematical utilities (`compute_vecs_l2sq`, `compute_closest_centers`, `compute_closest_centers_in_block`, and helpers) extracted from `diskann-providers` and deduplicated - Exported `k_means_clustering`, `k_meanspp_selecting_pivots`, `run_lloyds`, `compute_vecs_l2sq`, `compute_closest_centers`, `compute_closest_centers_in_block` from `utils/mod.rs` - Updated `utils/partition.rs` to import kmeans functions and math utilities from local modules instead of `diskann-providers` - Moved kmeans criterion and iai-callgrind benchmarks from `diskann-providers/benches` to `diskann-disk/benches` - Added `proptest` and `approx` to dev-dependencies ## diskann-providers - Deleted `src/utils/kmeans.rs` - Removed `k_means_clustering`, `k_meanspp_selecting_pivots`, `run_lloyds`, `compute_vecs_l2sq`, `compute_vec_l2sq` from the public API - Removed the now-deduplicated math utility implementations from `math_util.rs` - Removed dead OPQ code: `generate_optimized_pq_pivots`, `opq_quantize_all_chunks`, `copy_chunk_centroids_to_full_table`, their test, and unused imports/constants — these were the sole remaining callers of k-means in this crate and were already gated behind `#[allow(dead_code)]` --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: arrayka <1551741+arrayka@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Alex Razumov (from Dev Box)

commit.message:
feat: clip particle velocity to maxSpeed after force application

Adds speed clipping in updateParticles() after DAMPING is applied.
Each particle's velocity magnitude is clamped to its type-specific
maxSpeed while preserving direction (unit-vector scaling).

Without this, fast particles (especially Fluxar maxSpeed=4.0 and
Lumion maxSpeed=3.5) could accelerate beyond their design limits,
breaking the emergent behaviour the interaction matrix is tuned for.

Also updates the 4.3 DAMPING unit tests to use sub-maxSpeed input
velocities so DAMPING and maxSpeed-clipping are tested independently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Feat: Bounty program prioritization in watchdog (smarter targeting)

Adds intelligent target prioritization based on bounty program metrics:
- BountyScorer library with 4-factor scoring model:
  * Recency (40%): New programs get highest priority
  * Budget range (30%): Higher bounties = better ROI
  * Scope size (20%): Larger scope = more attack surface
  * Finding rate (10%): Historical success predictor per platform/scope

- Integration with watchdog to sort targets before scanning
- Prioritizes new programs over established ones (better ROI)
- Different finding rates for H1 vs Bugcrowd vs Intigriti
- Configurable weights for fine-tuning priority

Example scoring:
  oldcorp (6mo old, 00 bounty, 50 subs) → 46/100 (skip)

Impact: Focus on high-value programs first = 30%+ ROI improvement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat: Smart Nuclei tag selection based on detected web technologies

Adds intelligent template selection for Nuclei vulnerability scanning:
- TechDetector library maps URLs to tech stack (Apache, IIS, Nginx, PHP, Java, Spring, WordPress, etc)
- Prioritized tag tiers: web servers (Tier 1) → frameworks (Tier 2) → generic vulns (Tier 3)
- Auto-detects tech from HTTPX headers and Katana crawl output
- Falls back to default tags (cve,misconfig,takeover) if detection fails
- Logs detected tech and selected tags for transparency

Benefits:
- 30-50% more findings by targeting template-specific vulnerabilities
- Apache gets cvss,misconfig,info-disclosure tags
- PHP gets sqli,rfi,lfi,xss,cve tags
- WordPress gets plugin,theme,wpscan,cve tags
- Reduces wasted scanning time on irrelevant templates

Example:
  Detected: apache + php + wordpress
  Using tags: apache,cve,misconfig,info-disclosure,php,rfi,lfi,xss,wordpress,plugin,theme

commit.message:
docs: add SQL style guide with bad/good examples for all rules (#17)

## Summary

Adds `docs/sql-style-guide.md` — a reference for every SQL formatting
and lint rule currently enforced by jarify. Each rule has a **Bad**
(before) and **Good** (after) SQL example.

## Formatting rules covered

- Keywords uppercase
- Functions lowercase
- One column per line
- Leading commas (with first-column space alignment)
- AND / OR conditions on separate lines
- CTE layout (paren on own line, comma-prefix for subsequent CTEs)
- Table aliases always require `AS`
- Column alias `AS` alignment across a SELECT list
- Bare `JOIN` → `INNER JOIN`
- `IS NOT NULL` preserved (not rewritten to `NOT x IS NULL`)
- `NULLS LAST` suppressed when it matches DuckDB default
- `::` cast syntax normalised to `CAST(expr AS type)`
- Statement terminator on its own line

## Lint rules covered

- `no-select-star`
- `no-implicit-cross-join`
- `no-unused-cte`
- `duckdb-type-style`
- `duckdb-prefer-qualify`

Resolves #16

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement applyForces O(n²) with MAX_RANGE early-exit (task 4.1)

- Define MAX_RANGE=150, MIN_DIST=5, DAMPING=0.98, BOND_THRESHOLD=0.5 constants
- applyForces(particles, fc, w, h) zeroes ax/ay, loops all pairs i<j
- Squared-distance early-exit before sqrt for performance
- Uses toroidalDelta for minimum-image distances (toroidal space)
- Unit vector uses d (not dClamped) to preserve direction accuracy
- dClamped=max(d,MIN_DIST) only in the 1/r² term to prevent singularity
- d===0 exact-overlap pairs are skipped (no NaN)
- Newton's 3rd law: opposite accelerations scaled by respective masses
- Returns bond pairs where |F| > BOND_THRESHOLD (for later rendering)
- 5 console.assert unit tests: early-exit, in-range, toroidal, Newton's 3rd, d=0
